name: Braised Pork Loin with Turnip and Potatoes
recipeIngredient: 1 large (2 1/2 lb) turnip (rutabaga), peeled, quartered, sliced 1/3 inch thick
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F.
2. Wipe pork with damp paper towels. Mix 1 tsp salt, 1/2 tsp pepper, and the allspice; rub into surface of pork. Place pork, fat side up, in large roasting pan with cover. Roast, uncovered, for 1 hour.
3. Pour apple cider into bottom of pan. Add rutabaga slices. Roast, covered, for 1 hour.
4. Meanwhile, peel and quarter potatoes.
5. Remove roasting pan from oven. Add potatoes to pan drippings around roast. Baste vegetables with drippings; then sprinkle with 1 tsp salt and 1/5 tsp pepper.
6. Roast, covered, for 1 hour longer, or until turnip and potatoes are tender. If desired, sprinkle potatoes with paprika.
7. Remove roast and vegetables to heated platter; keep warm.
8. Skim off excess fat from drippings. Over direct heat, bring remaining drippings to boiling; boil, uncovered, for 15 minutes. Spoon over pork and vegetables.
